tactical_shape: 4-3-3 / attacking approach implied, with Kevin De Bruyne wide delivery and Leandro Trossard creating byline chances
Pushed forward and generated 15 shots, but were blunt in attack and struggled to break Iran down
standout_players: Thibaut Courtois — alert early, made an instinctive save from Taremi
Kevin De Bruyne — glittered in moments; the byline pass to De Cuyper was singled out as brilliant
Romelu Lukaku — huffed and puffed on his first start for club or country in more than 12 months
Nathan Ngoy — saw red after wiping out Mehdi Taremi
Maxim De Cuyper — twice involved in the biggest Belgium chances, but denied by Beiranvand
fatigue_injuries: Jérémy Doku was absent through illness
Romelu Lukaku was making his first start for club or country for more than 12 months
home_read.momentum: Pressure is mounting after another disappointing result; Belgium are in bother and must beat New Zealand to keep their hopes alive.

tactical_shape: Set up to absorb pressure and threaten in transition / from set-piece situations, with a long throw and organized defending
Stayed resilient under pressure and were willing to suffer without wilt
standout_players: Alireza Beiranvand — the moment of the match; made a miraculous save and had one of his best days
Amir Ghalenoei praised Beiranvand as one of Iran's greatest goalkeepers and extremely experienced, intelligent
Ali Nemati — stopped De Bruyne's cross with his legs splayed
Hossein Kanaanizadegan — forced Courtois into an alert save
Mehdi Taremi — had a precise finish ruled out for offside and won the red-card incident
fatigue_injuries: Sixteen hours to prepare in LA, two flights and a heavy game were cited as taxing conditions
away_read.momentum: Iran believe they can progress, with two creditable draws and growing confidence after resisting Belgium under severe conditions.
